response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile senior full-stack engineer, but their profile is a poor match for this embedded networking security role. They lack the core requirements: no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency, minimal C/UNIX systems programming (a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), and no demonstrated Linux kernel analysis or modification experience. There is no background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, MACsec, 802.1X, or embedded security technologies. Their expertise centers on web platforms, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang, which does not transfer to firmware development for LAN-switch products.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience (15+ years) across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture, and open-source contributions
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No indication of Japanese language proficiency, a hard requirement for the role
Very limited C/UNIX experience and no Linux kernel investigation or source modification background
No experience with networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF) or LAN-switch/embedded firmware products

key_factors.factor: Lack of C/Linux kernel and embedded networking expertise
Strong general engineering seniority and craftsmanship
